title: Validation rules for regulatory transactions in electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) format - Canada.ca
content:
## Validation rules for regulatory transactions in electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) format
January 10, 2025 Our file number: 24-111717-221
Health Canada has updated the validation rules for regulatory transactions submitted in the electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) format, to reflect recent and upcoming changes in our processes. These rules are built in accordance the information provided in the following documents:
- Guidance Document – Preparation of Drug Regulatory Activities in Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) Format;
- Creation of the Canadian Module 1 Backbone (eCTD) format – Guidance Document
- International Council for Harmonisation (ICH) Electronic Common Technical Document Specification (Version 3.2.2)
- Guidance Document: The Regulatory Enrolment Process (REP)
The purpose of the validation rules is to help ensure Sponsors provide a valid electronic transaction to Health Canada, and reduce errors and follow-up with Sponsors. Sponsors are encouraged to use a commercially available tool to validate their regulatory transactions in eCTD format, prior to filing them to Health Canada.
Health Canada validates each regulatory transaction as it is received. If the validation fails due to one or more errors detected, an eCTD Validation Report describing each error will be emailed to the sponsor as a .pdf file attachment.
Version of the eCTD validation rules: 5.3
Effective date: May 31, 2025
Should you have any questions regarding the content of this document, please email us at ereview@hc-sc.gc.ca .
F09 – Updated description G22 – Added new file extensions H08 – Changed valid values version v5 to v6 H21 – Added new rule I05 and I09 – Added Master file (MF XML)
F09 – Updated description G22 – Added new file extensions H08 – Changed valid values version v5 to v6 H21 – Added new rule I05 and I09 – Added Master file (MF XML)
Added Master File (MF) XML file to I02, I03, I06, I07 and I08 Updated text in B32, B44, B47, B48 (no change to the logic of the rule) B49 – Added searchable documents I09 – Changed dash to underscore I11 – Added version of the RT XML and MF XML files
Added Master File (MF) XML file to I02, I03, I06, I07 and I08 Updated text in B32, B44, B47, B48 (no change to the logic of the rule) B49 – Added searchable documents I09 – Changed dash to underscore I11 – Added version of the RT XML and MF XML files
F14 – Revised to exclude image files from this check F15 – Added new valid file extensions G22 – Added new valid file extensions G34 – Added new node extension R8 Product Lifecycle Management Information
Checks the sequence folder structure for any empty folders (folders without any files or subfolders).
File and Folder Security
Checks the sequence folder structure for any files or folders the user does not have access permission to.
This rule's implementation checks for files that exceed the configured maximum size. Warning for PDF files between 150 MB and 200 MB. Warning for other file types larger than 100 MB (excluding SAS XPT).
This rule's implementation checks for files that exceed the configured maximum size (Max. size: 200 MB for PDF, 1 GB for SAS XPT).
Sequence Folder Requirements
Checks the sequence folder name. Furthermore the rule will report a finding if the sequence seems to be the initial sequence for the application but its name is not 0000.
Higher sequences found
Reports error if the validated sequence is not the highest in the application folder.
XML Backbones Identification
Identifies all ICH and regional index files found in the sequence folder. Incorrect or invalid index files will be reported as errors.
Identifies all STF files found in the sequence folder. Invalid STF files will be reported as errors. STF files with an incorrect name will be also reported as errors.
Each subsequent transaction provided in the lifecycle of a dossier, must have a sequence number that is consecutively incremented by 1 number (for example 0000, 0001, 0002, 0003, and so on). Skipping numbers is not acceptable for sequence numbers. Validating a transaction with sequence number 0004 will report an error if sequence number 0003 does not exist (even if sequence 0004 does not have any reference to it).
Corrupt and password protected WORD documents
Word document will be reported as corrupted or password protected if one or more of the following situations occur: Document is corrupt or unreadable Document has password protection Document references an attached template located via http or https Document is empty Note: This rule applies only to Word version 2007 or later (.docx).
- Document is corrupt or unreadable
- Document has password protection
- Document references an attached template located via http or https
Note: This rule applies only to Word version 2007 or later (.docx).
Duplicate transaction
This rule checks if the eCTD sequence that is being validated already exists in the eCTD dossier.
Corrupt or unreadable PDF documents
A PDF document will be reported as corrupt if one or more of the following situations occur: The document cannot be opened because it is damaged. The document appears to have 0 pages. The document appears to be an application form, but the form content cannot be read. The document contains additional data (more than 1024 characters) after the last %%EOF (End Of File). An example of situation number (3) could be a document appearing to have 1 page only and the page contains the text phrase "Please wait... If this message is not eventually replaced by the proper contents of the document, your PDF viewer may not be able to display this type of document."
- The document cannot be opened because it is damaged.
- The document appears to have 0 pages.
- The document appears to be an application form, but the form content cannot be read.
- The document contains additional data (more than 1024 characters) after the last %%EOF (End Of File).
Bookmarks – Absolute (Rooted)
Retrieves all non-relative bookmarks in PDF documents and prints the total count, including the broken ones.
Bookmark – External (www, e-mail)
Retrieves all web link bookmarks and e-mail bookmarks in PDF documents and prints the total count.
Bookmarks – External (other)
Retrieves all external bookmarks other than web links and e-mail links from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Bookmarks – Inactive
Retrieves all inactive bookmarks in PDF documents and prints the total count.
Bookmarks – Inter Application, broken
Retrieves all broken inter-application bookmarks in PDF documents and prints the total count.
Bookmarks – Intra Application, broken
Retrieves all broken intra-application bookmarks in PDF documents and prints the total count.
Bookmarks – Intra Sequence, broken
Retrieves all broken intra-sequence bookmarks from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Retrieves all unknown bookmarks (links that could not be identified or contain JavaScript code) from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Reports the number of bookmarks per document and the total number of bookmarks in the sequence.
Hyperlinks – Absolute (Rooted)
Retrieves all non-relative hyperlinks from PDF documents and prints the total count, including the broken ones.
Hyperlinks – External (www, e-mail)
Retrieves all web links and e-mail links from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Hyperlinks – External (other)
Retrieves all external hyperlinks other than web links and e-mail links from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Hyperlinks – Inactive
Retrieves all inactive hyperlinks from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Hyperlinks – Inter Application, broken
Retrieves all broken inter-application hyperlinks from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Hyperlinks – Intra Application, broken
Retrieves all broken intra-application hyperlinks from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Hyperlinks – Intra Sequence, broken
Retrieves all broken intra-sequence hyperlinks from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Retrieves all unknown hyperlinks (links that could not be identified or contain JavaScript code) from PDF documents and prints the total count.
Reports the number of hyperlinks per document and the total number of hyperlinks in the sequence.
Finds PDF documents with password protection.
PDF version checking
Checks all PDF document versions against the list of allowed versions.
PDF Protection: Owner password
Finds all documents with an owner password set, excluding Health Canada application forms regardless of their location in module 1 and literature references in subfolders: 33-lit-ref, 43-lit-ref, 54-lit-ref.
Finds all documents with an owner password set, excluding Health Canada application forms regardless of their location in module 1 and literature references in subfolders: 33-lit-ref, 43-lit-ref, 54-lit-ref.
PDF Protection: Encrypted
Finds all encrypted documents.
Bookmarks – deep destination check
For all bookmarks, the named destination or destination page will be verified. Non existing destinations will be reported (i.e. the destination file exists but the page does not).
Bookmarks – multi action
Finds all bookmarks with multiple actions assigned.
Hyperlinks – deep destination check
For all hyperlinks, the named destination or destination page will be verified. Non existing destinations will be reported (i.e. the destination file exists but the page does not).
Hyperlinks – multi action
Finds all hyperlinks with multiple actions assigned.
PDF documents with attachments are not allowed
This rule is configured to detect attachments and portfolio documents found in PDF documents.
Bookmark does not 'Inherit Zoom"
All bookmarks and hyperlinks should have a magnification setting of "Inherit Zoom". This rule performs the corresponding checks for bookmarks.
Link does not 'Inherit Zoom'
All bookmarks and hyperlinks should have a magnification setting of "Inherit Zoom". This rule performs the corresponding checks for hyperlinks.
Checks for PDF documents with an incorrect initial view. ICH eCTD Specification: Documents with bookmarks must show the bookmarks pane in their initial view. The Magnification and Page Layout should be set as "default".
PDF documents with more than 10 pages must have bookmarks
PDF documents with more than 10 pages must have bookmarks. Excluded from this check are literature references in sections 3.3, 4.3, and 5.4; the life cycle management table; and Health Canada application forms regardless of their location in module 1.
PDF Protection – Printing
Finds all documents where printing is not allowed.
PDF Protection – Content Copying
Finds all documents where copying the content is not allowed.
PDF content restrictions
This rule checks for PDF files that contain, dynamic content (e.g., audio, video or special effects), or 3D content. Health Canada application forms are excluded from this check regardless of their location in module 1.
This rule checks for PDF files that contain, dynamic content (e.g., audio, video or special effects), or 3D content. Health Canada application forms are excluded from this check regardless of their location in module 1.
PDF content restrictions
This rule checks for PDF files that contain JavaScript. Health Canada application forms are excluded from this check regardless of their location in module 1.
This rule checks for PDF files that contain JavaScript. Health Canada application forms are excluded from this check regardless of their location in module 1.
Searchable documents
Document contains images only and is not text searchable
C – REFERENCED FILES
HREFs to targets outside application
Checks for broken links in submission files.
Hyperlinks targeting files outside the application will be identified.
HREFs to targets outside sequence
Checks for broken links in submission files.
Hyperlinks targeting files outside sequence will be identified.
Life Cycle Management Semantics
This check option refers to the current sequence. It will perform a general verification of the leaf life cycle properties in this sequence only (no deep history scanning).
For leaf elements with operation "new", the eValidator verifies that: modified file must not exist / title is given / href must exist.
For leaf elements with operation "replace", the eValidator verifies that: modified file must exist / title is given / href must exist.
For leaf elements with operation "append", the eValidator verifies that: modified file must exist / title is given / href must exist.
For leaf elements with operation "delete", the eValidator verifies that: modified file must exist / title is not required / href must not exist.
The eValidator verifies that for initial submissions all leaf elements have the operation "new".
The eValidator checks modified files in LCM: leaf in previous sequence must exist. It also verifies that when the attribute "modified-file" has been used for a leaf document then the referenced document exists in the previous submission. It is recognized that this attribute is invalid when used for submission sequence number 0000.
The eValidator checks that all files referenced in the backbone exist.
The eValidator regenerates the MD5 checksums for all referenced files and compares the checksums to the values provided in the backbone file.
Checks the syntax of all referenced file names (path length, illegal characters, etc.). The maximum path length is 200 characters.
The eValidator checks that only relative references (href and modified file) are used. Also, only forward slashes "/" are allowed (no backslashes).
Do the file references in the index files (ICH, Regional, STF) use relative paths? Absolute (i.e. rooted) paths are not allowed.
Searches for files not referenced in an index file (ICH, Regional or STF).
DTD/Schema Checksums
Rule compares the checksums of the DTD and schema against the following checksums:
ich-ectd-3-2.dtd: 1d6f631cc6b6357f0f4fe378e5f79a27
ca-regional-2-2-.xsd: ff564d6e69adebd9a9b4f274e65cf5f1
xml.xsd: 382b0a4f7529d2c5f7b0af0aa713b0a5
xlink.xsd: 52d1a3b8596e4fb61d3ec1cde24be16a
ich-stf-v2-2.dtd: 0972c10a4dadf3df5d2f41b2026a4a5c
Existence of Node extensions
The eValidator checks for "node-extension" elements being used and reports them for informational purposes.
MD5 checksums for index files will be calculated and compared against the MD5 checksums provided in the MD5 text files (not case sensitive).
MD5 checksums for index files will be calculated and compared against the MD5 checksums provided in the MD5 text files (not case sensitive).
Validate against delivered DTD
Performs XML validation for ICH Backbone and Regional backbones.
Uses the DTD or Schema given in the sequence number folder (util/dtd).
All files should have one and only one file extension
Checks if referenced files have exactly one file extension.
Element must have leaf
Checks for elements not having any leaf child nodes. This only affects elements with a name that starts with 'm'.
The folder m1\ca must exist.
The sequence folder must have a subfolder m1 with a subfolder ca.
No subfolders in ca-subfolder
The folder m1\ca must not have any subfolders.
Leaf title must not be empty
For operations other than 'delete', all leafs must have a 'title' child. The title must be present.
Module 1 (regional xml file) exists
The regional backbone ca-regional.xml file must exist in folder m1\ca.
Application folder name must match dossier-identifier
The element value for the dossier-identifier must match the name of the parent folder of the sequence folder. And the dossier-identifier must start with the letter e or s.
Element sequence-description
Refer to the Regulatory Transaction Descriptions document available upon request on the Filing Submissions Electronically . Applies only to Clinical Trial Application (CTA), Clinical Trial Application – Amendment (CTA-A), Pre-Clinical Trial Application Meeting (pre-CTA), and Development Safety Update Report (DSUR).
Refer to the Regulatory Transaction Descriptions document available upon request on the Filing Submissions Electronically .
Applies only to Clinical Trial Application (CTA), Clinical Trial Application – Amendment (CTA-A), Pre-Clinical Trial Application Meeting (pre-CTA), and Development Safety Update Report (DSUR).
Cover letter operation attribute
Cover letter should have 'new' operation attribute.
Multiple operations on same document in same sequence
Checks for documents in the regional backbone, which are used as modified-file more than once.
Checks for file reuse scenarios (Same values for xlink:href used multiple times in the regional backbone for this sequence).
Replace should not provide identical content to the previous file
When replacing content, the new content should be different from the previous content. This check does not apply to image files provided in module 1.
When replacing content, the new content should be different from the previous content. This check does not apply to image files provided in module 1.
Invalid file extension
Valid file extensions include: Documents: .pdf, .doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx, .wpd, .ppt, .pptx Images: .png, .gif, .svg, .jpg, .jpeg, .tif, .tiff, .bmp Audio/Video: .wav, .mp3, .mp4, .wmv, .mov, .mpg, .mpeg Coding Language: .xml Data: .dat, .inf, .txt.
Valid file extensions include:
Documents: .pdf, .doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx, .wpd, .ppt, .pptx
Images: .png, .gif, .svg, .jpg, .jpeg, .tif, .tiff, .bmp
Audio/Video: .wav, .mp3, .mp4, .wmv, .mov, .mpg, .mpeg
Coding Language: .xml
Data: .dat, .inf, .txt.
Detect invalid life cycle: Delete
Detect invalid life cycle pattern: Delete operations causing branches.
Detect invalid life cycle: Replace
Detect invalid life cycle pattern: Replace operations causing branches.
Detect invalid life cycle: Delete
Detect invalid life cycle pattern: Operation on deleted leaf content.
Element sequence-number
The value of the sequence-number element must match the sequence folder name.
Operation for Life Cycle Management Table
The operation attribute for this leaf element must be "new" when provided for the first time, and "replace"/ "delete" whenever referenced again as part of a subsequent transaction.
The operation attribute for this leaf element must be "new" when provided for the first time, and "replace"/ "delete" whenever referenced again as part of a subsequent transaction.
Product Name and Applicant
Element values product-name and applicant must not be empty.
Cover letter should not have more than three pages.
Node extensions in module 1
Node extensions in module 1 backbone (ca-regional.xml) are not allowed, except in 1.2.6 Authorization for Sharing Information heading, 1.2.7 International Information heading, and 1.6.1 Comparative bioavailability Information heading. The validator checks all headings and subheadings in the Canadian regional backbone (ca-regional.xml). It generates an error when there is (are) node extension(s) found in any subheadings other than those mentioned above. This rule does not apply to leaves with operation "delete".
Node extensions in module 1 backbone (ca-regional.xml) are not allowed, except in 1.2.6 Authorization for Sharing Information heading, 1.2.7 International Information heading, and 1.6.1 Comparative bioavailability Information heading.
The validator checks all headings and subheadings in the Canadian regional backbone (ca-regional.xml). It generates an error when there is (are) node extension(s) found in any subheadings other than those mentioned above. This rule does not apply to leaves with operation "delete".
Node Extensions in 1.2.7 International Information
Node extensions are expected in section 1.2.7 International Information heading. Note: There is a mixture of node extensions and leaves allowed in the 1.2.7 International Information heading. A warning is generated when there is a (are) leaf (leaves) added under this heading. The warning serves the purpose to notify the user to manually verify the leaves added under this heading. Requirements are outlined below and will result in failure of the transaction if not followed: When provided, the International Status document and the Foreign review attestation must be added as leaves directly under 1.2.7 subheading. Any other foreign agency (such as FDA, EMA) related documents must be provided using node extensions. This rule does not apply to leaves with operation "delete".
Node extensions are expected in section 1.2.7 International Information heading.
Note: There is a mixture of node extensions and leaves allowed in the 1.2.7 International Information heading. A warning is generated when there is a (are) leaf (leaves) added under this heading. The warning serves the purpose to notify the user to manually verify the leaves added under this heading. Requirements are outlined below and will result in failure of the transaction if not followed:
When provided, the International Status document and the Foreign review attestation must be added as leaves directly under 1.2.7 subheading. Any other foreign agency (such as FDA, EMA) related documents must be provided using node extensions. This rule does not apply to leaves with operation "delete".
Node Extension title must not be empty
For node extensions, the title child must be present (value must be present).
Use of operation append
The use of operation "append" is not allowed in module 1.
The use of operation "append" is not allowed in module 1.
G – ICH BACKBONE 3.2
All files should have one and only one file extension
Checks if referenced files have exactly one file extension.
Attribute checksum-type
The checksum-type attribute must have the value md5 or MD5.
Attribute dosage form
Checks if the attribute value is present.
Checks if the attribute value is present.
Attribute Indication
Checks if the attribute value is present.
Attribute Manufacturer
Checks if the attribute value is present. Applicable only to sections where this attribute is mandatory, otherwise reported as warning.
Attribute Product-Name
Checks if the attribute value is present.
Checks if the attribute value is present. Applicable only to sections where this attribute is mandatory, otherwise reported as warning.
Element must have leaf
Checks for elements not having any leaf child nodes. This only affects elements with a name that starts with 'm'.
File index.xml exists
The index.xml file must be present.
File index-md5.txt exists
The MD5 checksum file index-md5.txt must be present.
The m1 folder must be present.
The util folder must be present.
Leaf title must not be empty
For operations other than 'delete', all leaves must have a 'title' child. The title must be present.
m1-administrative element must exist
The element m1-administrative-information-and-prescribing information must be present.
No other files in m1
The m1 folder must not have any other files in addition to the files explicitly allowed.
No other files in root
The root folder (sequence number folder) must not have any other files in addition to the files explicitly allowed.
Node Extension title must not be empty
For node extensions, the title child must be present (value must be present).
Regional backbone(s) referenced operations
Regional backbone(s) referenced (ca-regional.xml) should have 'new' operation attribute.
Multiple operations on same document in same sequence
Checks for documents in the ICH backbone, which are used as modified-file more than once.
Checks for file reuse scenarios (Same values for xlink:href used multiple times in the regional backbone for this sequence).
Invalid file extension
Valid file extensions include: Documents: .pdf, .doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx, .wpd, .ppt, .pptx Images: .png, .gif, .svg, .jpg, .jpeg, .tif, .tiff, .bmp Audio/Video: .wav, .mp3, .mp4, .wmv, .mov, .mpg, .mpeg Coding Language: .xml, .xsl, .xsd, .dtd Data, Modeling and Simulation: .dat, .inf, .txt, .sas, .xpt, .wksz, .wksx, .wks (SimCyp workspace files, Pharmaceutical Drugs only) .sdax, .edpdp, .wsp, .epr, .pnf, .psf, .sprx (Lot Release Program for Schedule D drugs, Biologic Drugs only) .mdb, .pbk, .dsd, .opd, .spd, .mdd, .ipd, .zfd, .cdd, .crd, .psd, .tpd, .efd, .itd, .stc, .psa, .beq, .ivc, .cat, .iap, .imp, .iop, .dlp, .plp, .ocp (GastroPlus files, Pharmaceutical Drugs only)
Valid file extensions include: Documents: .pdf, .doc, .docx, .xls, .xlsx, .wpd, .ppt, .pptx
Images: .png, .gif, .svg, .jpg, .jpeg, .tif, .tiff, .bmp
Audio/Video: .wav, .mp3, .mp4, .wmv, .mov, .mpg, .mpeg
Coding Language: .xml, .xsl, .xsd, .dtd
Data, Modeling and Simulation: .dat, .inf, .txt, .sas, .xpt, .wksz, .wksx, .wks (SimCyp workspace files, Pharmaceutical Drugs only)
.sdax, .edpdp, .wsp, .epr, .pnf, .psf, .sprx (Lot Release Program for Schedule D drugs, Biologic Drugs only)
.mdb, .pbk, .dsd, .opd, .spd, .mdd, .ipd, .zfd, .cdd, .crd, .psd, .tpd, .efd, .itd, .stc, .psa, .beq, .ivc, .cat, .iap, .imp, .iop, .dlp, .plp, .ocp (GastroPlus files, Pharmaceutical Drugs only)
Replace or append should not provide identical content to the previous file
When replacing or appending content, the new content should be different from the previous content.
Leaf count under a node
The number of leaves directly under a single node must not exceed 1000. This rule applies only to module 5 when using node extensions and only to leaves with operation attribute "new".
Detect invalid life cycle: Append
Detect invalid life cycle pattern: Append operations causing branches.
Detect invalid life cycle: Append
Detect invalid life cycle pattern: Append operations not appending to most recent STF leaf.
Detect invalid life cycle: Delete
Detect invalid life cycle pattern: Delete operations causing branches.
Detect invalid life cycle: Replace
Detect invalid life cycle pattern: Replace operations causing branches.
Detect invalid life cycle: Delete
Detect invalid life cycle pattern: Operation on deleted leaf content.
Detect invalid life cycle: Append
Detect invalid life cycle pattern: Append on Append.
Strength in dosage form attribute
Do not provide strength values in the dosage form attribute in sections 2.3.P, 3.2.P, 3.2.A.1, or 3.2.A.2. Any numeric value found in these attribute values will be reported as an error. If the strength value was already present in a previous sequence, the rule will not report an error for the current sequence.
Do not relocate content
This applies to leaf elements in the ICH sections modules 2-5. When revisions are sent to a regulatory authority, the new leaf element should be submitted in the same location in the backbone as the leaf element being appended, replaced or deleted.
SAS XPT dataset files (XPORT)
This check will examine the SAS XPT dataset files (XPORT). Files that are corrupt or compressed will be reported as Error. For details about the technical specifications, please refer to the FDA study data technical conformance guide.
Node extensions in 3.2.R Regional Information
Leaf elements in 3.2.R Regional Information heading must be provided using node extensions. PDF files are not allowed as leaf elements directly under 3.2.R Regional Information heading. Acceptable titles of the node extensions are: 3.2.R.1 Production Documentation 3.2.R.2 Medical Devices 3.2.R.3 Lot Release Documentation 3.2.R.4 Yearly Biologic Product Report 3.2.R.5 Assessment of Similarity 3.2.R.6 On Site Evaluation 3.2.R.7 Other Regional Information 3.2.R.8 Product Lifecycle Management Information Warning will be reported if the naming of the title is not followed. Notes: The requirements above apply only to leaves provided using operation attribute "New". They don't apply to leaves provided using operation attribute "Replace". The exact naming convention of the titles of node extensions must be used when the node extension(s) is (are) created for the first time under this heading. If new or replaced leaves are provided when there are already existing node extensions in the 3.2.R Regional information heading, the leaves must be provided under the existing node extensions, even if they don't follow the exact naming convention of the node extension title. Warning generated during the validation can be ignored.
- 3.2.R.1 Production Documentation
- 3.2.R.2 Medical Devices
- 3.2.R.3 Lot Release Documentation
- 3.2.R.4 Yearly Biologic Product Report
- 3.2.R.5 Assessment of Similarity
- 3.2.R.6 On Site Evaluation
- 3.2.R.7 Other Regional Information
- 3.2.R.8 Product Lifecycle Management Information
Warning will be reported if the naming of the title is not followed.
Notes: The requirements above apply only to leaves provided using operation attribute "New". They don't apply to leaves provided using operation attribute "Replace".
The exact naming convention of the titles of node extensions must be used when the node extension(s) is (are) created for the first time under this heading.
Check Index Reference
Verifies that the href target exists and has the correct value.
Check Index Reference (title – match)
Verifies that the href target exists and matches the title value.
Content Block are not accepted
Reports any used content-block element.
No backslash in HREF
Verifies that href values do not contain backslashes.
Study Identifier category must not be empty
The value of the study-identifier/category element must not be empty.
Study Identifier study-ID must not be empty
The value of the study-identifier/study-id element must not be empty.
Study Identifier title must not be empty
The value of the study-identifier/title element must not be empty.
Categories and file tags
Checks file tag values and category values against definitions in valid-values.xml file. Accepting values as defined in valid-values.xml v6.
STF leaf element must reference other STF leaf upon append
STF leaf element must reference other STF leaf upon append.
Such leaf elements must not reference PDF files as modified files.
Category information must be provided for certain STFs
Category information must be provided for certain STFs.
STF cannot reference another STF
Leaf references in STFs must always target content files, not STFs.
STF does not relate to any leaf elements
STF files must reference at least one leaf.
Study ID for STF must remain constant
The STF study IDs must not change in the application life cycle.
Invalid STF TOC location
STFs should only be associated with certain headings under Modules 4 or 5.
STF doc-content file tag count
There should be one and only one file tag for each doc-content.
Case Report Form(s) structure in 5.3.7
If Study Tagging Files are used in the current sequence being validated, the node 5.3.7 must not be used. The Case Report Forms must be referenced from the Study Tagging Files. The previous sequences in the same dossier will not be checked.
Use Study Tagging Files or Node Extensions
Leaf elements in module 4 (except those in subsection 4.3), with an operation attribute "new", can use either node extensions, STFs or may be placed directly under the TOC sections. Leaf elements in subsections 5.3.1, 5.3.2, 5.3.3, 5.3.4, 5.3.5, and 5.3.7, with an operation attribute "new", can use only node extensions or STFs. Although both node extensions and STFs are acceptable for study reports, only one or the other approach should be used consistently throughout the lifecycle of a leaf. Also, in a specific sequence all leaf elements should use the same approach. Note : Previous sequences in the same dossier will not be checked, and neither will leaf elements with operation attributes other than "new".
Leaf elements in module 4 (except those in subsection 4.3), with an operation attribute "new", can use either node extensions, STFs or may be placed directly under the TOC sections.
Leaf elements in subsections 5.3.1, 5.3.2, 5.3.3, 5.3.4, 5.3.5, and 5.3.7, with an operation attribute "new", can use only node extensions or STFs.
Although both node extensions and STFs are acceptable for study reports, only one or the other approach should be used consistently throughout the lifecycle of a leaf. Also, in a specific sequence all leaf elements should use the same approach.
Note : Previous sequences in the same dossier will not be checked, and neither will leaf elements with operation attributes other than "new".
Valid STF DTD version
Verifies the DTD version in the STF XML file
I: Regulatory Enrolment Process (REP) and XML files
An XML file will be reported as corrupt if the file cannot be opened because the file is damaged.
Missing Regulatory Transaction (RT) XML file or Master File (MF) XML file
Checks the 1.2.1 Application Forms node. Generates error if the RT file is missing from the current eCTD sequence and was provided in the previous eCTD sequence. Note: This rule is not based on the presence of the RT file in the current sequence. This rule is based on the existence of the RT XML file or MF XML in the previous eCTD sequence. Error must not be generated if there is no RT xml or MF XML file present in the previous eCTD sequence.
Checks the 1.2.1 Application Forms node. Generates error if the RT file is missing from the current eCTD sequence and was provided in the previous eCTD sequence.
Note: This rule is not based on the presence of the RT file in the current sequence. This rule is based on the existence of the RT XML file or MF XML in the previous eCTD sequence. Error must not be generated if there is no RT xml or MF XML file present in the previous eCTD sequence.
Placement of the Regulatory Transaction (RT), Product Information (PI) or Master File (MF) xml files
Verifies the placement of the RT, PI or MF xml files. Generates error if they are placed in a node other than 1.2.1 Application Forms.
Presence of the Submission Application form (3011)
Checks the 1.2.1 Application Forms node. Generates error if, in addition to the RT xml file, there is a 3011 form named hc-sc-3011-en.pdf or hc-sc-3011-fr.pdf.
Checks the 1.2.1 Application Forms node. Generates error if, in addition to the RT xml file, there is a 3011 form named hc-sc-3011-en.pdf or hc-sc-3011-fr.pdf.
Presence of other xml files in the Application Forms node
Checks the 1.2.1 Application Forms node. Generates error if in addition to the RT xml and PI xml or MF XML file there are other xml files. Error will also be reported if more than one RT or MF XML file is found in this node.
Checks the 1.2.1 Application Forms node. Generates error if in addition to the RT xml and PI xml or MF XML file there are other xml files. Error will also be reported if more than one RT or MF XML file is found in this node.
Comparison of the RT or MF file with the top level folder (Dossier ID folder)
Compares the value in the Dossier ID field in the RT or MF xml file with the top level folder.
Compares the value in the Dossier ID field in the RT or MF xml file with the top level folder.
Operation Attribute Value for the RT or MF XML file
The operation attribute value in the leaf element must be "new" when provided for the first time, and "replace" when provided in subsequent transaction.
The operation attribute value in the leaf element must be "new" when provided for the first time, and "replace" when provided in subsequent transaction.
Dossier ID Naming convention
Checks the Dossier ID field in the RT or MF XML file. It must be lower case letter "e" followed by 6 digits.
Checks the Dossier ID field in the RT or MF XML file. It must be lower case letter "e" followed by 6 digits.
Product Name and company ID in the RT and MF XML file
Element values for "product_name" and "company_id" must not be empty.
Version of the RT XML and MF XML files
Checks the software_version element value of the Regulatory Transaction (RT) and Master File (MF) XML files. For the latest versions of the RT and MF XML files accepted by Health Canada refer to: REP Summary Table – for Human Use Drugs (pharmaceutical, biologic and radiopharmaceutical), disinfectants, veterinary drugs and medical devices Filling submissions electronically – for master files
Checks the software_version element value of the Regulatory Transaction (RT) and Master File (MF) XML files.
